spec.goal: Add vstyle enforcement for module documentation placement and unnecessary crate-level `use ... as _;` keep-alive imports.
success_criteria: vstyle reports a violation when a module declaration is documented with outer `///` or `/** */` comments instead of module-internal `//!` or `/*! */` comments.
vstyle reports a violation when a crate-level `use foo as _;` keep-alive import exists without a justified need to keep the dependency alive.
Existing trait keep-alive import behavior under RUST-STYLE-IMPORT-003 remains intact.
Rule catalog and focused regression tests cover the new behavior.
constraints: Keep the persisted plan artifact inside this active workspace lane.
Do not rely on Clippy for these two policies; implement them in vstyle.
Preserve current alias and trait keep-alive semantics outside the new crate keep-alive rule.
Use repo-native focused verification after implementation.

tasks.title: Implement new rule checks and tests
tasks.objective: Add vstyle detection and fix behavior for module doc placement and unnecessary crate keep-alive imports, with targeted tests.
outputs: Regression tests covering valid and invalid cases
verification: Focused Rust tests for the touched rule families pass.

tasks.title: Update surfaced rule catalog
tasks.objective: Document the new rule ids and behavior in the repo rule catalog so the CLI-exposed coverage and docs stay aligned.
outputs: README rule catalog updated for the new rule ids
verification: Coverage output includes the new rules and README descriptions match implemented behavior.

tasks.title: Run focused verification and finalize runtime state
tasks.objective: Validate the saved plan, run focused repo-native verification for the new rules, and record evidence in plan state.
verification: Plan validates with the plan-writing validator.

triggers: A new rule needs a different id topology or shared utility extraction that changes the task graph.
Focused verification exposes a semantic conflict with existing import or module rules.
Implementation reveals that external-file modules and inline modules need separate policy decisions.

evidence: cargo run --quiet --bin vstyle -- coverage | rg 'MOD-004|IMPORT-012' -> both rules implemented
cargo test --quiet mod004_ -> ok (4 passed) after narrowing MOD-004 to comment-style outer docs only
cargo make lint-vstyle -> success after MOD-004 review-finding repair
cargo test --quiet import012_ -> ok (4 passed) after widening IMPORT-012 to same-package evidence
cargo make lint-vstyle -> success after IMPORT-012 cross-file review-finding repair
